creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
videos.summary: Isadora Martin-Dye presents a four-layer architecture for maintaining brand voice in LLM outputs: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. This layered approach is designed to prevent voice degradation across extended interactions, illustrated with examples from wedding venue, personal AI companion, and missing persons support tools.

videos.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
videos.summary: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS that converts an Obsidian vault of 10,000+ notes, documents, and repositories into structured context for AI agents. The system uses a three-layer architecture of raw content, indexing, and a wiki layer to enable efficient memory and context engineering without requiring vector or graph databases.

videos.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
videos.summary: Gabe De Mesa from OpenGov shares experience building and scaling OG Assist, an AI agent product serving thousands of state and local governments. The session covers production agent architecture including core loop design, feedback mechanisms, long context handling, monitoring, and enterprise tooling patterns using Claude and Effect-TS.

videos.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
videos.summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents fail because they operate with severe context limitations and memory loss between interactions, analogous to an expert engineer who can only see a tiny portion of code at a time. He advocates for solutions like Nx and Polygraph to enable agents with better workspace awareness and continuity.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin reviews Google's 50-page whitepaper on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model itself is only 10% of success while the surrounding harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) comprises 90%. The video covers the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering, context engineering as a core skill, and the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles in AI-assisted development.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is a memory layer that persists across multiple AI agents and tools, storing encrypted memories owned by the user. It enables Claude and other AI providers to share context without direct integration between them.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code is an open-source, coding-focused agentic model from Moonshot AI. The reviewer emphasizes strong performance relative to its size and cost.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: AI engineers are building loops that automatically prompt agents repeatedly instead of manually prompting each request. This approach uses orchestrator agents to dispatch parallel worker agents, reducing manual intervention but increasing token costs and compounding hallucination risks. Observability and harnesses are required to run such systems reliably on smaller models.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses a new project related to OpenClaw, featuring a GitHub repository with skills for setting up Crabbox and a codebase harness. The project appears aimed at developers interested in AI engineering and code generation workflows.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: Loop Engineer is a technique for building and configuring AI agents. The content includes a GitHub repository with codebase harness and knowledge template, plus a workshop offered through AI Builder Club.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explaining how to construct proactive AI agents and self-improving systems. Covers building autonomous loops, demonstrates use cases including autonomous ads optimization and SEO, and discusses tools and memory systems that enable such architectures.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses emerging autonomous coding tools and features, including /goal functionality and mission exploration capabilities. The video covers how these features work, setup, and prompting strategies for achieving long-horizon coding tasks.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: An educational video explaining the fundamental mechanics of Generative Pretrained Transformers (GPT), covering self-attention, multi-head attention, positional embeddings, feed-forward networks, layer normalization, and residual connections. The video walks through how these components work together in a Transformer decoder block.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code presents a tutorial on bigram language models, a simple but instructive approach to understanding how tokens are processed in language models. The video covers tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, backpropagation, and optimization as core components leading toward understanding GPT and attention mechanisms.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3, a series of language models including Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The models use a hybrid architecture combining Mamba, transformer layers, Latent Mixture of Experts, and Multi-Token Prediction.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model implementing MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) to replace full attention mechanisms. The model optimizes KV cache access patterns and uses tiled I/O improvements to reduce latency during prefill and decode stages, addressing infrastructure bottlenecks between HBM and SRAM.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents a seven-step framework for system design interviews: Requirements, traffic estimation, high-level components, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints. The approach emphasizes understanding the problem before choosing technology, using a URL shortener as a concrete example.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: This video tutorial explains how AI agents use tool calling to move beyond guessing and access real-time data. It covers why LLMs need tools, how tool calling works with model requests and framework execution loops, tool composition with schema and handlers, and practical implementation using AWS Strands Agents and the MCP standard.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS supplies AI coding agents with tools, up-to-date documentation, best practices, and guardrails for working with AWS services. It improves agent success rates and reduces effort by providing reliable procedures instead of relying on outdated or incomplete general knowledge.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers demonstrates running Claude Code, Anthropic's agentic coding tool, on Amazon Bedrock to enable data residency and compliance while building features and automating development tasks. The walkthrough covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and Guardrails governance.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines whether Google engineers use code generation tools, comparing vibing practices between OpenAI Codex and Claude Code. The video discusses code generation adoption at Google across 2024-2026 timelines.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: A video discussing Malus, a company that the creator argues is "ending open source." The video covers open source dynamics, an AI agent incident, and the future challenges for open source maintainers, framed as commentary on disruption to the open source ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that code alone does not create sustainable software moats. Instead, customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational complexity provide durable competitive advantages. He contends that fears of AI displacing SaaS companies are overstated.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan, a Stanford roboticist, argues that the humanoid robot race led by companies like 1X and Unitree represents a narrow vision for robotics. She contends that designing for human interaction and delight, rather than utility alone, will be critical as billions of robots enter society.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how to compete against incumbent enterprise software giants by building a full platform from day one rather than shipping single features. She argues that even the most innovative companies like Google rely on spreadsheets for planning and explains Pigment's hiring philosophy and trust-based growth strategy for enterprise sales.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, discusses how to distinguish real product-market fit from false signals driven by passionate niche customers. He argues that true PMF manifests as customer pull rather than founder push, and advises embedding in customer workflows instead of conducting formal interviews while being cautious about overconfident bets on AI model trajectories.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Could Delete EVERYTHING! Businesses Ignored This! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses overlooked security threats in agentic AI systems, including prompt injection attacks, unauthorized data deletion, and unintended actions. The video argues that businesses remain hesitant to adopt agentic AI due to these risks despite the technology's potential benefits.

videos.title: AI Podcast: Anthropic's Latest & Lisbon's Electric Vibe #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof's "This Is Tech Year" podcast episode covers Anthropic's latest announcements and includes guest discussion. The episode blends tech news coverage with cultural context from Lisbon.

videos.title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
videos.summary: Small teams using agile approaches accomplish tasks more efficiently, while large teams suffer from excessive meetings and reduced productivity. The claim is that efficiency improves with team size reduction.

videos.title: AI Memory Boom: Micron & SK Hynix Surge! Huge Growth Ahead! #shorts
videos.summary: The video discusses how AI workloads are driving increased demand for high bandwidth memory. Micron and SK Hynix are positioned as key players shaping future memory architecture for AI systems.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
